confirmed.title: Boss summoned adds survive encounter reset and stack across wipes
confirmed.detail: When a boss with summonAdds (Vael the Mistcaller, Grand Necromancer Velkhar) resets after a wipe, mob.firedSummons is reset to 0 (sim.ts:1836, and respawnMob:1906) but the adds it already summoned are never despawned — instance mobs never respawn/despawn until freeInstance, and live adds just evade back to the boss spawn. On the next pull the thresholds fire again and spawn a fresh wave on top of the leftovers. Reproduced with a script harness: pull Vael to 50% -> 2 drowned_thralls; wipe, wait for full reset (boss idle, full hp, firedSummons 0) -> the 2 thralls are still alive; re-pull to 50% -> 4 thralls alive. Every wipe permanently adds +2 (Vael) or +3 (Velkhar) elite-pull adds, making the encounter monotonically harder after each wipe — the opposite of a reset — and effectively soft-locking a struggling party until the 300s empty-instance timeout wipes their entire dungeon progress. The leftover adds also re-grant XP each cycle (slow farm loop). Note adds are also missing cleanup in respawnMob, which matters if any overworld mob ever gets summonAdds (adds with overworld spawnPos would additionally start respawning forever via the respawnTimer path).
confirmed.fix: Track summoned add ids per boss (e.g., add a summonedIds: number[] field set in spawnBossAdds) and despawn/delete those entities (clearing player target/combo refs like freeInstance does) wherever the encounter resets: the evade-arrival block (sim.ts:1829-1839) and respawnMob (sim.ts:1900-1911), before zeroing firedSummons.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by code trace and live repro. spawnBossAdds (src/sim/sim.ts:1937-1960) creates adds with spawnPos = boss.spawnPos inside the instance; instance mobs are never respawned/despawned outside freeInstance (updateMob 1713-1722; freeInstance 2869-2885, only after 300s empty per INSTANCE_EMPTY_TIMEOUT). On a wipe, retargetMob (1671-1681) sends boss and adds to evade; the evade-arrival reset (1828-1839) zeroes boss.firedSummons but never despawns the live adds, and updateBossMechanics (1918-1935) re-fires the thresholds on the next pull. respawnMob (1893-1913) has the same gap. Reproduced with a scratch vitest harness against the branch code: pull Vael to 50% -> 2 drowned thralls; real wipe (boss kills player, releaseSpirit) and full reset (boss idle, 1490/1490 hp, firedSummons 0) -> the 2 thralls still alive; re-pull to 50% -> 4 thralls ({"afterPull1":2,"afterReset":2,"afterPull2":4}). Encounters with summonAdds (Vael, Velkhar) therefore get monotonically harder after every wipe instead of resetting. Two minor imprecisions in the finding (leftover adds grant XP only once each — the farm loop comes from fresh waves; leftover adds aren't socially pulled with the boss since social pull is same-templateId only at 1691-1692, but they idle-proximity-aggro at the boss spawn anyway) do not change the verdict. HIGH, not CRITICAL: a party can still recover by leaving the instance empty for 300s, at the cost of all dungeon progress. The proposed fix direction (track summoned add ids and despawn them in the evade reset and respawnMob, clearing player target/combo refs like freeInstance) is sound.

confirmed.title: Boss enrage/summon log events broadcast to every player on the server
confirmed.detail: The new boss mechanics emit plain log events with no pid and no entity anchor: spawnBossAdds emits { type: 'log', text: `${boss.name} calls for aid!` } and updateBossMechanics emits `${mob.name} becomes enraged!`. GameServer.eventAnchor() only scopes world events that carry a targetId/entityId; anchorless events are deliberately broadcast (the comment says 'chat/log etc'). Result: every player on the server — questing in zone 1, standing in town — gets 'Korzul the Gravewyrm becomes enraged!' and 'Grand Necromancer Velkhar calls for aid!' in their chat log every time any party anywhere hits a summon threshold (2 per Vael/Velkhar pull) or enrage (Korgath, Korzul). With 18 instance slots across 3 dungeons this fires constantly on a busy server, and also leaks other parties' encounter progress. The companion 'aura' and 'spellfx' events on the same lines are correctly anchored to the mob; only the log lines leak.
confirmed.fix: Give these log events an anchor so the existing EVENT_RADIUS routing scopes them: add an optional entityId to the log SimEvent variant and set it to the boss id in updateBossMechanics/spawnBossAdds (eventAnchor already checks `'entityId' in ev`). Alternatively emit one pid-scoped log per player within EVENT_RADIUS of the boss. Note the pre-existing global '${mob.name} returns home.' at sim.ts:1786 has the same problem and could take the same fix.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed end-to-end by code trace and a runtime check. The new boss log emissions (src/sim/sim.ts:1932 'becomes enraged!' and :1940 'calls for aid!', both introduced in this branch) carry no pid and no targetId/entityId; the log SimEvent variant (src/sim/types.ts:452) has no anchor field at all. GameServer.routeEvents (server/game.ts:347-364) delivers any pid-less event whose eventAnchor (game.ts:366-372) is null to every connected session, and the server runs a single shared Sim (game.ts:70). The client prints every log event to chat (src/ui/hud.ts:768). A scratch vitest reproduced it: after claiming a gravewyrm_sanctum instance and dropping Korzul below the 30% enrage threshold, sim.tick() emitted {"type":"log","text":"Korzul the Gravewyrm becomes enraged!","color":"#ff6666"} with no anchor, and the replicated routeEvents logic delivered it to a player 2369.8 units away (EVENT_RADIUS=90), while the companion 'aura' event on the adjacent line was correctly scoped (delivered near, withheld far) — confirming only the log lines leak. Velkhar's 'calls for aid!' behaved identically. Supporting claims check out: INSTANCE_SLOT_COUNT=6 x 3 dungeons = 18 slots; the ' returns home.' broadcast pre-exists on main (main:src/sim/sim.ts:1749). Severity HIGH is appropriate: every player on a multiplayer server gets other parties' encounter spam in chat repeatedly, plus a cross-party encounter-progress leak — player-visible wrong behavior, though not a crash/exploit. The proposed fix (optional entityId on the log variant, set to the boss id) slots directly into the existing eventAnchor check.

confirmed.title: Shadow-only proxy layer never renders: rig shadows pop off entirely at 25u
confirmed.detail: The mid-range shadow system assumes the sun's shadow camera uses its own layer mask: shadowProxy meshes get `layers.set(SHADOW_ONLY_LAYER)` (layer 1) and the code enables layer 1 on `sun.shadow.camera.layers`. But in three r165 the shadow pass filters objects against the MAIN render camera's layers, not the shadow camera's: WebGLShadowMap.renderObject does `object.layers.test(camera.layers)` where `camera` is the camera passed to `renderer.render()` (verified in node_modules/three/src/renderers/webgl/WebGLShadowMap.js:179,335 and WebGLRenderer.js:1181). The main camera only has layer 0 enabled, so every shadowProxy fails the test in BOTH passes and is never drawn anywhere. Net effect on high/ultra: articulated casters get castShadow=false past ENTITY_SHADOW_RANGE (25u) in sync(), the proxy that was supposed to take over (25-62u per ENTITY_PROXY_SHADOW_RANGE_SQ) silently does nothing, so every NPC/mob/player rig loses its shadow with a visible pop at 25u — exactly the grounding the comment at lines 28-31 says the proxy exists to preserve. The proxy meshes/geometry are pure dead weight per entity.
confirmed.fix: Drop the layers trick. Put the proxy on the default layer with a shared shadow-only material (e.g. clone rigMergedMat once with `colorWrite: false; depthWrite: false`) and keep `castShadow = true`: the shadow pass swaps in its depth material so the shadow renders, while the main pass rasterizes nothing visible (one cheap draw per proxy, matching the intended budget). Alternatively keep one material and toggle `colorWrite` via `proxy.onBeforeRender`/`onAfterRender`. Add a screenshot test at ~40u to lock the behavior, since this shipped unnoticed.

verdict.reasoning: CONFIRMED. The code at src/render/renderer.ts:188 enables layer 1 on sun.shadow.camera.layers, and shadowProxy meshes are placed on layer 1 (line 449), assuming the shadow pass culls against the shadow camera's layer mask. But in the installed three r165, WebGLRenderer.js:1181 calls shadowMap.render(shadowsArray, scene, camera) with the MAIN render camera, and WebGLShadowMap.js:335 tests object.layers.test(camera.layers) against that main camera (shadowCamera is only used for matrices). The main camera's layers are never modified anywhere in the codebase (grep confirms the only layers usage is these three lines), so its mask stays 1 while the proxy's mask is 2 — the test fails in both the shadow pass and the main color pass (WebGLRenderer.js:1288). A scratch script run with the project's own three package confirms: shadow-pass test vs main camera = false, the assumed test vs shadow camera = true, masks proxy=2/mainCam=1/shadowCam=3. The post pipeline (post.ts:75 RenderPass) uses the same main camera, so there is no path where the proxy renders. End-to-end effect: sync() (lines 774-781) sets castShadow=false on all articulated casters past 25u and enables the proxy, which silently does nothing — every NPC/mob/player rig shadow pops off entirely at 25u on all non-low tiers, while rigs remain visible to 80u and the shadow box covers 95u (props/trees in that band keep shadows, making the pop conspicuous). This defeats the explicit design intent in the comment at lines 27-31, and the proxy meshes are dead weight per entity. Player-visible rendering bug, not harmless: HIGH severity stands. The proposed fix (default layer + shared colorWrite:false material, keep castShadow=true) is sound since the shadow pass swaps in its own depth material.

confirmed.title: removeView never disposes per-entity GPU resources — leaks on online interest churn and every dungeon run
confirmed.detail: Every EntityView owns unique GPU resources: finalizeRig() and buildFarRig() in src/render/models.ts create per-entity merged BufferGeometries (clone+mergeGeometries per rig, again for the far LOD, again lazily for sheep/bear form rigs), and the object branch of createView allocates per-view materials and textures (`new MeshLambertMaterial` for crate/lid at lines 388-393, stone + portal materials and Circle/Box geometries for doors at 361-383, and a brand-new `sparkleTexture()` canvas + SpriteMaterial per loot object at 397). removeView only does `scene.remove(group)` — no `geometry.dispose()` / `material.dispose()` / `texture.dispose()` anywhere. Entity churn is constant: the online client prunes everything outside the server's INTEREST_RADIUS=120 every snapshot (src/net/online.ts:322-326, server/game.ts:270), so walking the 1080u strip creates/destroys full rig+farLOD geometry sets continuously; offline, every dungeon run despawns ~20-40 instance mobs plus the exit portal (sim.ts freeInstance). three r165 holds GL buffers in WeakMaps, so VRAM is only reclaimed if/when the JS GC happens to collect the wrappers (GC sees none of the GPU pressure), and `renderer.info.memory.geometries/textures` grows without bound — the standard three.js leak pattern. Re-entering interest also re-runs the expensive clone+merge rig build on the main thread, causing hitches when several mobs cross the boundary in one snapshot.
confirmed.fix: In removeView, traverse v.group and dispose owned resources: merged rig geometries, farMesh.geometry (shared with shadowProxy — dispose once), sheep/bear rig geometries, and for object views the crate/portal geometries. Do NOT dispose shared cached materials (rigMergedMat/surfaceMat). Better: hoist the crate/lid/door-stone/portal materials, crate geometry and sparkleTexture() to module-level shared singletons so views stop owning them at all. Longer term, cache merged rig geometry by (templateId|color|form) so same-template mobs share one geometry — fixes both the leak and the re-entry hitch.

verdict.reasoning:
CONFIRMED, and the mechanism is actually worse than the finding states — for geometry it is a hard, GC-unreclaimable leak, not a GC-lazy one.

Verified end-to-end:

1. removeView leaks (renderer.ts:737-745): it does only `scene.remove(v.group)`, nameplate removal, and a clickTargets splice. The only `dispose()` call in the entire renderer is the PMREM generator at init (line 165). No geometry/material/texture disposal anywhere in src/render.

2. Per-view unique GPU resources confirmed: finalizeRig (src/render/models.ts:166 `mesh.geometry.clone().applyMatrix4(rel)`, :175 `mergeGeometries`) and buildFarRig (:207, :210) create fresh merged BufferGeometries for every entity view; sheep/bear form rigs are built lazily per view (renderer.ts:817-826). Object views allocate per-view stone/portal materials + Box/Circle geometries (renderer.ts:361-383), crate/lid materials + geometries (:387-394), and `sparkleTexture()` (textures.ts:846-867) builds a brand-new canvas + CanvasTexture on every call — it is not memoized — so every loot crate view owns a unique GL texture. Rig materials are shared via rigMatCache (so the proposed "don't dispose shared materials" caveat is correct).

3. Churn paths confirmed: online client deletes every entity absent from the snapshot (src/net/online.ts:322-325; server sends only within INTEREST_RADIUS=120, server/game.ts:7,270), and renderer.sync removes views for missing entities (renderer.ts:757-758), so walking the world continuously creates/destroys full rig+farLOD sets. Offline, freeInstance (src/sim/sim.ts:2869-2880) deletes all instance mobs plus the exit portal per dungeon run. (One nuance: offline overworld respawns reuse the same Entity object — respawnMob mutates in place — so steady-state offline overworld play does not churn; the leak rate offline is dominated by dungeon runs, exactly as the finding says.)

4. The key adversarial question — "is this just GC-lazy and therefore harmless?" — is answered NO by the installed three 0.165.0 source: WebGLBindingStates stores VAOs in a plain object keyed by geometry.id (node_modules/three/src/renderers/webgl/WebGLBindingStates.js:7, getBindingState :73-99), removed only by releaseStatesOfGeometry (:504-528), which only runs from the geometry 'dispose' event handler (WebGLGeometries.onGeometryDispose). Worse, saveCache (:182-226) stores strong references to the geometry's BufferAttributes (`data.attribute = attribute`), which pins the merged typed arrays in JS heap forever AND keeps the WebGLAttributes WeakMap keys alive, so the GL vertex/index buffers (deleted only in attributes.remove, WebGLAttributes.js:130-138) are never freed. So for every entity geometry that rendered at least once (anything within ENTITY_DRAW_RANGE=80), removeView leaks VAOs + GL buffers + JS typed arrays permanently for the renderer's lifetime (renderer created once, src/main.ts:25). renderer.info.memory counters also grow without bound.

5. Quantified with a scratch run (vite-node, project's own buildRigFor/buildFarRig over 45 mob templates, low-gfx material path with DOM stubbed): avg 8.4 unique geometries and ~47 KB of attribute data per entity view, i.e. ~4.6 MB of permanently pinned attribute data plus ~840 leaked geometries (each with 1-2 VAOs and GL buffers) per 100 entity churns. A dungeon run (~20-40 mobs + portal) leaks ~1-2 MB; an hour of online play with constant interest churn plausibly leaks tens of MB of pinned heap + VRAM, growing without bound. The re-entry hitch claim is also true: re-entering interest re-runs the full clone+merge rig build per entity with no template-level cache.

Severity stays HIGH: real, unbounded, player-visible degradation in long sessions (memory growth, eventual GPU pressure/crash on constrained devices, hitches on interest churn), but it requires extended play to hurt and causes no data loss or exploit, so not CRITICAL. The proposed fix (dispose owned geometries/textures in removeView, hoist shared object materials/geometry/sparkle texture to module singletons, optionally cache merged rigs by template) is correct; the only caveat confirmed is to dispose the farMesh geometry once (shared with shadowProxy) and never dispose the cached rigMergedMat/surfaceMat materials.

confirmed.title: Idle 'breathing' accumulates head Y position for kobold/skeleton/murloc rigs — frame-rate-dependent floating heads
confirmed.detail: `parts.head.position.y = (activeRig.kind === 'humanoid' ? 2.18 : parts.head.position.y) + Math.sin(this.time * 1.8 + e.id) * 0.012` — for biped rigs whose kind is NOT 'humanoid' (kobold, skeleton, murloc — all common mob families, skeletons fill the new Gravewyrm Sanctum) this ADDS the sine term to the current position every frame instead of setting an absolute value. The discrete integral of sin at 60fps gives an oscillation of roughly ±0.5-0.8 units (≈0.012/sin(Δ/2)), nearly ±1u at 144Hz — idle mobs' heads visibly drift up off the neck and back, and the magnitude depends on frame rate. The same line exists on main, so it pre-dates this branch, but the branch rewrote this sync path and ships far more skeleton/murloc content, so it should be fixed here.
confirmed.fix: Record the rest height at build time (e.g. `rig.headRestY = head.position.y` in models.ts, or a per-kind lookup) and assign absolutely: `parts.head.position.y = rig.headRestY + Math.sin(this.time * 1.8 + e.id) * 0.012`. The '2.18' literal for humanoids should come from the same captured rest value.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed end-to-end. renderer.ts:858 reads `parts.head.position.y = (activeRig.kind === 'humanoid' ? 2.18 : parts.head.position.y) + Math.sin(this.time * 1.8 + e.id) * 0.012` — for non-humanoid biped rigs this is self-referential (`y += sin(...)*0.012` per rendered frame, not dt-scaled). It is the ONLY write to head.position.y in the renderer; rig builders set head Y once at construction (skeleton 2.12, murloc 1.28, kobold via humanoid), so nothing ever resets the accumulation. buildSkeleton/buildMurloc/buildKobold all have leftLeg/rightLeg (so they enter the biped branch at line 846) and parts.head, and content declares 12 undead + 2 murloc + 2 kobold mob templates (family undead → buildSkeleton). A scratch simulation (/tmp/headdrift.mjs) of 60s idle reproduced the exact math: skeleton head (rest 2.12) wanders to y∈[2.018, 2.818] at 60fps and y∈[1.880, 3.800] at 144fps; murloc head sinks from 1.28 to 0.483 at 60fps — versus the intended ±0.012 breathing amplitude. Heads visibly float off necks / sink into torsos on every idle skeleton/murloc/kobold, frame-rate dependent. The identical line exists on main (line 701) so it pre-dates the branch as the finding disclosed, but the branch rewrote renderer.ts (~858 changed lines) and triples skeleton/murloc content, so it should be fixed here. Proposed fix (capture rest head Y at build time, assign absolutely) is correct. Not CRITICAL (no crash/exploit/data loss), but a constantly player-visible wrong-behavior bug: HIGH.

confirmed.title: q_sexton quest rewards are unequippable for 6 of 9 classes (archetype fallback vs single-class lock)
confirmed.detail: The new Hollow Crypt quest q_sexton (src/sim/content/zone1.ts:330-340) rewards via itemRewards { warrior: 'marrowtread_boots', mage: 'sextons_slippers', rogue: 'gravewalker_softboots' }. Reward resolution uses the archetype fallback (src/sim/sim.ts:2296: itemRewards[cls] ?? itemRewards[REWARD_ARCHETYPE[cls]]), so paladins/shamans receive marrowtread_boots, priests/warlocks/druids receive sextons_slippers, and hunters receive gravewalker_softboots. But these three new items are locked requiredClass: ['warrior'] / ['mage'] / ['rogue'] respectively, and equipItem (src/sim/sim.ts:2058-2061) rejects them with 'You cannot equip that.' Net effect: 6 of the 9 classes complete a 5-man dungeon quest and get blue boots they can never wear. The branch authors knew about this exact trap — zone2.ts:491-495 defines WAR=['warrior','paladin','shaman'], MAG=['mage','priest','warlock','druid'], ROG=['rogue','hunter'] with a comment saying 'the lock must admit the whole group', and every zone-2/zone-3 class-locked reward uses those groups — but the new items added to items.ts missed the fix.
confirmed.fix: In src/sim/content/items.ts widen the locks to the archetype groups used by zone2/zone3: marrowtread_boots → ['warrior','paladin','shaman'], sextons_slippers → ['mage','priest','warlock','druid'], gravewalker_softboots → ['rogue','hunter'] (or drop requiredClass entirely, like the existing crypt blues gravecaller_blade/widowfang_dirk/gravecaller_staff). Consider extracting the WAR/MAG/ROG constants from zone2.ts into a shared module and also applying them to the pre-existing zone-1 rewards (redbrook_blade, apprentice_staff, keen_dirk, militia_vest, woven_robe, shadow_jerkin) which have the same latent problem. Add a content test: for every quest, the resolved reward for each of the 9 classes must be equippable by that class.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed end-to-end with both static tracing and an executed check. q_sexton (src/sim/content/zone1.ts:337) rewards only warrior/mage/rogue keys; sim.ts:2296 resolves rewards for the other 6 classes via REWARD_ARCHETYPE (data.ts:83-87), handing paladin/shaman marrowtread_boots, priest/warlock/druid sextons_slippers, hunter gravewalker_softboots. Those three items (items.ts:153-164, new in this branch) are locked requiredClass ['warrior']/['mage']/['rogue'], and both equipItem (sim.ts:2058-2061, 'You cannot equip that.') and maybeAutoEquip (sim.ts:2133) reject them. The items have no other source (quest-reward only). A scratch vitest in /tmp mirroring the exact sim logic reproduced all 6 broken class/reward pairs for q_sexton, and also confirmed the finding's secondary claim that q_bandits/q_ringleader have the same pre-existing issue on main (verified via git show main:src/sim/data.ts). zone2.ts:491-495 contains the WAR/MAG/ROG group constants with a comment explicitly stating the lock must admit the whole archetype group, proving the new items deviate from the intended pattern. Real player-visible bug: 6 of 9 classes complete a 5-man dungeon quest and receive a rare item they can never equip. HIGH severity is appropriate (wrong behavior players will hit, not a crash/exploit). The proposed fix (widen locks to archetype groups or drop requiredClass like the sibling crypt blues) is correct.

confirmed.title: Korzul / Velkhar 'one of three' drops are independent rolls — ~30% of final-boss kills drop no epic, ~26% drop two or more
confirmed.detail: master-spec.md §4 specifies 'Korzul → one of three EPIC weapons (wyrmfang_greatblade / staff_of_the_gravewyrm / fang_of_korzul, ~1/3 each)' and Velkhar → 'one of three blues'. The content encodes this as three LootEntry rows with chances 0.34/0.33/0.33 (sum 1.0), but rollLoot (src/sim/sim.ts:1647-1658) rolls every entry independently. With independent 0.34/0.33/0.33 rolls: P(zero epics) = 0.66×0.67×0.67 ≈ 29.6%, P(exactly one) ≈ 44.5%, P(two or more) ≈ 25.9%. So roughly 3 in 10 full clears of the expansion finale award no epic at all from the level-20 capstone boss, while a quarter award multiple — both contradict the spec's guaranteed-one design. Same math applies to Velkhar's three blues (0.33/0.33/0.34). Dungeon mobs stay dead until the instance resets (sim.ts:1717-1719), so a no-drop clear means a full re-run for another chance.
confirmed.fix: Implement exclusive loot groups: e.g., add `rollGroup?: string` to LootEntry and in rollLoot perform a single rng.next() per group, partitioning [0,1) by the entries' chances so exactly one entry in the group drops (weights summing to 1.0 keep the existing data unchanged). Alternatively special-case in content with a single weighted pick for boss 'choice' tables. Add a test asserting Korzul/Velkhar always drop exactly one item from their three-way table.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed end-to-end. (1) Data: dungeons.ts:121-126 (Velkhar: boneguard_breastplate 0.33 / shadowmeld_tunic 0.33 / staff_of_velkhar 0.34) and dungeons.ts:136-141 (Korzul: wyrmfang_greatblade 0.34 / staff_of_the_gravewyrm 0.33 / fang_of_korzul 0.33) — chances sum to exactly 1.0, indicating intended weighted-exclusive pick. (2) Engine: rollLoot (sim.ts:1647-1658) rolls rng.chance(entry.chance) independently per entry (rng.ts:21-23 is `next() < p`); LootEntry (types.ts:92-97) has no group field and grep over src/sim and server finds no exclusive-loot logic anywhere. (3) Spec: master-spec.md:268 explicitly states 'Velkhar → one of three blues' and 'Korzul → one of three EPIC weapons (..., ~1/3 each)'. (4) Empirical: scratch vitest in /tmp importing the real Rng class and real MOBS data, 1M simulated kills per boss, gave Korzul P(0 items)=0.2967, P(1)=0.4437, P(2)=0.2226, P(3)=0.0370 (Velkhar nearly identical) — matching the finding's claimed ~29.6%/~44.5%/~25.9%. (5) Impact amplifier confirmed: sim.ts:1717-1719 keeps dungeon mobs dead until instance reset, so a no-drop clear of the level-20 capstone forces a full re-run. This is a real, frequent, player-visible wrong behavior (3 in 10 finale clears award no epic; 1 in 4 award multiple), not a harmless technicality. Proposed fix (exclusive roll group with single partitioned rng draw, or weighted single pick for boss choice tables, plus a test) is sound and keeps existing data unchanged.

confirmed.title: The Drowned Chapel road runs through the middle of a deep-carved lake — ~50yd forced swim on the zone-2 mainline route
confirmed.detail: The Fenbridge → Drowned Chapel road spoke [(4,308) → (50,380) → (90,420)] passes within 7-10yd of the center of the 25-radius lake at (60,380). The lake carve in src/sim/world.ts:79-87 fully sinks terrain to WATER_LEVEL−4 inside radius×0.55 (13.75yd) and blends out to radius×1.6, so the road bed is ~4yd underwater for roughly 45-50 yards (it enters deep water ~18yd before the (50,380) waypoint and exits ~31yd after). This is the designed route for the q_drowned / q_drowned_censers / q_no_rest chain and the Widow Thicket spur, so every player follows it repeatedly: they're forced to swim (0.65× move speed, sim.ts:40), cannot eat/drink while swimming (sim.ts:2079), and any pursuing non-murloc mob stops dead at the waterline (sim.ts:1884), creating leash-cheese and combat weirdness exactly on the quest path. The renderer will also paint road texture under the lake. The conflict is inherited verbatim from master-spec.md §2 (the spec's own road point (50,380) sits inside its own lake (60,380,r25)), so this needs a content fix, not just a spec-conformance fix.
confirmed.fix: Reroute the spoke around the lake's south-east shore, e.g. ZONE2_ROADS[1] = [(4,308), (40,355), (62,410), (90,420)] (all points ≥ ~30yd from (60,380)), or shrink/move the lake (e.g. {x:55,z:372,radius:14}). Add a content test mirroring the zone-band test: for every road polyline segment, sampled points must satisfy terrainHeight ≥ WATER_LEVEL − SWIM_DEPTH so roads can never dip into deep water.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed empirically. ZONE2_ROADS[1] waypoint (50,380) at src/sim/content/zone2.ts:40 is 10yd from the lake center (60,380,r25) at line 21 — inside the full-carve radius (r*0.55=13.75yd). world.ts:79-87 sinks that terrain to WATER_LEVEL-4=-8.5 and no road-raising term or bridge prop exists. A scratch vitest run sampling the real heightfield with the production seed 20061 shows 49.5yd of the 142yd road below the swim threshold (-5.3), with the waypoint itself at -8.5 (4yd underwater); deep water spans ~19yd before to ~29yd after the waypoint, matching the finding. Player impact verified in sim.ts: isSwimming (568-570) triggers 0.65x speed (line 600), eat/drink blocked while swimming (2079), and non-murloc mobs halt at the waterline (1882-1884), enabling leash-cheese on the q_drowned/q_drowned_censers/q_no_rest quest route to the Drowned Chapel. Renderer paints road dirt with no underwater guard (terrain.ts:152-160), visually leading players into the lake. Only nit: "forced swim" is slightly strong since players can detour, but the road is the designed quest-route guide, so this is a real player-visible content bug. HIGH stands.

confirmed.title: Town music never starts on a fresh spawn — initial MusicZone sentinel equals the real starting zone
confirmed.detail: MusicDirector initializes `private zone: MusicZone = 'town'` and all layer gains/targets to 0. `update()` early-returns when `zone === this.zone && inCombat === this.combat`. Every offline game starts the player in Eastbrook hub, so the HUD's first call is `music.update('town', false)` — which matches the initial state and returns without ever raising the town layer's target. Result: total silence at spawn (despite the music toggle being on) until the player first leaves the hub radius+10 or enters combat. This pre-exists on main, but this branch rewrote the layer loop in update() and shipped the multi-zone soundtrack, and the new-player path (always spawning in the town theme zone) hits it every session.
confirmed.fix: Initialize the sentinel so the first update always applies, e.g. `private zone: MusicZone | null = null;` (and adjust the comparison), or after building layers in init() set `this.zone = null` / immediately apply targets for the current state on the first update() call.

verdict.reasoning: CONFIRMED by code trace and an executed scratch test against the real module. Constructor sentinel at src/game/music.ts:316-317 is zone='town', combat=false; init() creates all layers with gain 0 and target 0 (lines 363, 366); update() at line 384 early-returns when args match the sentinel. The HUD (src/ui/hud.ts:376-379) passes 'town' whenever the player is within hub.radius+10 of the zone hub, and a fresh spawn at PLAYER_START {x:2,z:-2} (src/sim/data.ts:106, sim.ts:250) is 2.8 units from the Eastbrook hub (0,0) with TOWN_RADIUS=26 — so the first call is exactly update('town', false), which no-ops, leaving every layer target 0; tickScheduler (line 412) then schedules no notes. Verified empirically with vitest + stubbed WebAudio: update('town', false) on a fresh director left layers.town.target === 0, while the control update('vale', false) set target 1. One narrowing nuance: hud.ts:48 lastCombatEventAt=0 makes inCombat spuriously true for the first 5000ms after page load, so a player entering the world within 5s of navigation bypasses the sentinel — but that path is effectively unreachable online (login/char-select/snapshot wait) and rare offline, and it only 'rescues' the music by wrongly playing combat percussion at spawn. The bug pre-exists on main (same sentinel, main music.ts:220/286), but this branch rewrote update() and ships the soundtrack as a feature, so it is in scope. Player-visible on every normal fresh session: total silence at spawn despite music enabled, until leaving the radius-36 town circle or first combat. Proposed null-sentinel fix is correct.

mediums.title: leaveDungeon has no in-dungeon guard — falls back to teleporting the player to the Hollow Crypt door
mediums.detail: leaveDungeon(pid) computes `const dungeon = dungeonAt(p.pos.x) ?? DUNGEON_LIST[0]` and unconditionally teleports the player to that dungeon's doorPos. If the player is not inside any instance (pos.x <= DUNGEON_X_THRESHOLD, where dungeonAt returns null), the ?? fallback silently teleports them across the map to the Hollow Crypt entrance at (80, 90). Current network/UI callers happen to gate on proximity to a dungeon_exit object (server/game.ts:252, src/main.ts:108/125), but the method is public on the Sim/world API and the ungated legacy alias leaveCrypt(pid) (sim.ts:2842-2844) is deliberately kept for tests and scripts — any present or future call while outdoors performs a free cross-zone teleport (e.g., a level-1 character in Eastbrook calling world.leaveDungeon() lands at the crypt door; from Highwatch it skips both ridge passes). The same `?? DUNGEON_LIST[0]` pattern can also misfire for any x in (600, 780) outside every dungeon's actual band.
mediums.fix: Early-return (optionally with an error event) when the player is not in an instance: `const dungeon = dungeonAt(p.pos.x); if (!dungeon) return;` — removing the DUNGEON_LIST[0] fallback. The addPlayer restore path (sim.ts:245) can keep its fallback since it already checked savedPos.x > DUNGEON_X_THRESHOLD.

mediums.title: Summoned boss adds survive encounter reset and stack across wipes
mediums.detail: When a party wipes, the boss evades and resets cleanly (hp = maxHp, firedSummons = 0, enraged = false), but the drowned_thrall / raised_bonewalker adds it summoned are independent mobs that just evade back to the boss's spawnPos and stay alive — nothing despawns them on encounter reset. firedSummons being reset means every retry summons a fresh wave at 66%/33%, so each failed Velkhar attempt permanently adds 3-6 more bonewalkers to the room (Vael: 2-4 thralls) that social-aggro into the next pull. The only cleanup is freeInstance, which requires the instance to be empty of players for INSTANCE_EMPTY_TIMEOUT = 300s — so a struggling party's encounter gets monotonically harder with no in-game explanation, on the level-20 finale bosses specifically. The code comment ('thresholds fire once per pull and reset on evade') shows the intent was a clean per-pull reset, which the leftover adds violate.
mediums.fix: Track summoned add ids on the boss (e.g., a summonedIds array, or tag adds with the boss id) and delete/despawn them in the evade-arrival branch (sim.ts:1830-1838) and in respawnMob, reusing the target-clearing loop from freeInstance so player targetId/comboTargetId are dropped before the entities.delete.

mediums.title: sellItem has no vendor-proximity, dead, or in-combat check — players can vendor from anywhere, including mid-dungeon
mediums.detail: buyItem validates an npc id, vendorItems membership, and dist2d <= INTERACT_RANGE + 2; sellItem validates only ownership and non-quest kind. Any client can send {cmd:'sell'} from anywhere, at any time — inside a dungeon between boss pulls, while dead, or while in combat — converting loot to copper with no return trip. To answer the exploit question directly: it is NOT a money-printing exploit even with the new vendor whites — I dumped all 143 items and verified no item has sellValue >= buyValue, conjured_water/2/3 all sell for 0c, equipped items can't be sold (countItem checks bags only), and quest items are blocked. The code is byte-identical to main, so this is pre-existing; but the expansion widens its impact: dungeons now drop meaningful vendorable loot (90c-500g copper plus whites/greens), and selling mid-instance removes the intended inventory-pressure/return-to-town loop that the new vendor economy is built around.
mediums.fix: Mirror buyItem's gate in sellItem: require a vendor NPC (one with vendorItems.length > 0) within INTERACT_RANGE + 2, and reject while p.dead. The HUD already only shows the sell UI at vendors, so a server-side check changes nothing for legitimate clients.

mediums.title: enter_dungeon / leave_dungeon accepted from dead players — corpse teleport and instance claim while dead
mediums.detail: Neither the server command handlers nor sim.enterDungeon/leaveDungeon check e.dead (the walk-in trigger path is gated by `if (!p.dead)` in tick(), but the explicit cmd path is not). A player who dies within 8yd of a dungeon door can send {cmd:'enter_dungeon'} and have their corpse teleported inside; if their party key has no instance yet this claims one of the dungeon's 6 instance slots and spawns the full 13-21 elite mob set for a player who cannot fight, tying the slot up until the 300-second empty timeout frees it. The reverse (dead near an exit, leave_dungeon) teleports the corpse to the overworld door, which also changes which graveyard releaseSpirit sends them to (zoneAt of door vs dungeon mapping). No duplication or progression exploit, but it is state the sim never expects (dead entities being repositioned and claiming instances) and trivially reachable by any scripted client.
mediums.fix: Add a dead check at the command boundary in server/game.ts (`if (!e || e.dead) break;` in both cases), or at the top of sim.enterDungeon/leaveDungeon next to the existing resolve() guard so the offline and headless paths get the same protection.

mediums.title: Shadow proxy ignores polymorph/bear form — humanoid standing shadow under sheep/bear in the 25-62u band
mediums.detail: `v.shadowProxy.visible = !wantShadow && d2 < ENTITY_PROXY_SHADOW_RANGE_SQ` is computed before the form-swap logic and never considers `polyed`/`bear`. A polymorphed or bear-form character between 25u and 62u renders the articulated sheep/bear rig (its casters have castShadow=false past 25u) while the shadow map draws the humanoid far-LOD proxy in its frozen standing pose — a person-shaped shadow under a sheep. The proxy pose-mirror at lines 920-923 copies the sheep rig's body transform onto the humanoid proxy, compounding the mismatch. Currently masked by the layer bug (finding 1), but it surfaces the moment that is fixed.
mediums.fix: Include the form state in the proxy gate: compute `polyed`/`bear` before the LOD/shadow block (or restructure) and set `v.shadowProxy.visible = !wantShadow && d2 < ENTITY_PROXY_SHADOW_RANGE_SQ && !polyed && !bear`; while a form is active, let the sheep/bear casters keep castShadow within the proxy band instead (they are 1-2 draws anyway).

mediums.title: Auto-detected low tier (software GL) still creates the context with antialias: true
mediums.detail: `new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ canvas, antialias: urlForcedTier() !== 'low', ... })` — when no URL tier is forced, antialias is true; the SwiftShader/llvmpipe autodetect in initGfxTier() only runs after the context exists. So the exact machines the low tier exists for (software GL: headless test runners, VMs, no-GPU laptops) end up direct-rendering onto a 4x-MSAA default framebuffer — the single most expensive setting for a software rasterizer, and inconsistent with the explicit `?gfx=low` path which disables it. Conversely on high/ultra the MSAA default framebuffer is wasted memory anyway, because all real rendering happens in the composer's own MSAA HalfFloat target and the final pass is a fullscreen blit.
mediums.fix: Decide AA before context creation without the live context: default `antialias: false` (high/ultra get their AA from the composer's MSAA target, low is meant to run without AA), or probe software GL on a throwaway 1x1 context first and pass the resolved tier into the WebGLRenderer options.

mediums.title: Per-frame allocations and stale pixel ratio in the sync path
mediums.detail: Three small per-frame costs in code this branch added: (1) `for (const id of [...this.views.keys()])` allocates an N-element array every frame just to prune leavers (N = hundreds offline). (2) budgetFireLights() allocates a fresh `{ light, d2 }` object per fire light per frame and sorts the list; fireLights grows as dungeon interiors are built (14-16 torches per interior x up to 12 instance slots plus all campfires, ~200 lights late-session), so this is ~12k short-lived objects/second at 60fps plus an O(N log N) sort, plus `getWorldPosition` matrix walks per light — steady GC churn in the hottest loop. (3) The resize handler never refreshes the pixel ratio: `setPixelRatio` is only called in the constructor, so dragging the window between monitors with different devicePixelRatio leaves the canvas, composer and vfx point-scale at the old DPI (blurry on the way up, over-rendered on the way down).
mediums.fix: (1) Iterate `this.views` directly and collect doomed ids into a reused scratch array. (2) Keep parallel pooled arrays (or mutate persistent entries) for the light ranking instead of rebuilding objects, and skip the sort when light count <= GFX.maxPointLights; cache each light's world position once at build time (they never move). (3) In the resize handler re-run `this.webgl.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, GFX.pixelRatioCap))` before setSize (EffectComposer.setPixelRatio likewise).

mediums.title: Combat ostinato keeps the previous zone's key when crossing a zone border mid-combat
mediums.detail: `combatLayer.transpose = COMBAT_TRANSPOSE[zone]` is only assigned inside `if (combatLayer.target !== combatTarget)`, i.e. when combat starts or stops. If the player crosses a zone boundary while staying in combat (being chased across the vale→marsh border at z=180 is the canonical case), the zone themes crossfade but the combat ostinato keeps the old zone's transpose — e.g. an A-rooted ostinato over the E-aeolian marsh theme, exactly the dissonance COMBAT_TRANSPOSE exists to prevent. With 4 distinct tonal centers now (0/7/2/5) the clash is much more audible than the old town/wilds pair.
mediums.fix: Update the transpose whenever the zone changes while combat is active, e.g. before the target check: `if (inCombat) combatLayer.transpose = COMBAT_TRANSPOSE[zone];` (notes are scheduled per-note via playNote, so the change takes effect on the next scheduled note).

mediums.title: Zone-crossing banner and welcome log lines have no hysteresis — straddling a border spams the HUD and rebuilds the map canvas
mediums.detail: The zone-transition check fires on the raw sign of `zoneAt(p.pos.z)` vs `lastZoneId`. A player fighting, kiting, or wandering along a band boundary (z=180 or z=540) re-triggers `showBanner` plus TWO gold log lines on every single crossing; the combat log keeps only 11 lines, so oscillating across the line flushes actual combat/loot messages and the banner stomps level-up/quest banners. Additionally, if the map window is open, each crossing invalidates `mapZoneId` and re-renders the 280x280 terrain canvas synchronously (~25ms measured for 78k terrainHeight+roadDistance samples) — a frame hitch per crossing.
mediums.fix: Add a dead-band: only commit the zone change when the player is some distance past the boundary (e.g. compute candidate = zoneAt(p.pos.z) and require |p.pos.z - boundary| > 5 before switching lastZoneId), or debounce re-announcement of a zone visited in the last N seconds. The mapBg regen is then naturally debounced too.

mediums.title: Map window re-derives the dungeon's zone from magic numbers instead of the existing dungeonAt() helper
mediums.detail: `updateMapWindow` resolves which zone map to show while inside an instance with `DUNGEON_LIST.find((d) => p.pos.x < 900 + d.index * 600 + 300)` — hard-coding the instance layout that lives in `instanceOrigin()` (x = 900 + index*600) in src/sim/data.ts, which also already exports `dungeonAt(x)` doing exactly this band lookup. If instance origins/spacing ever change (or a dungeon interior grows past ±300 in x), the HUD silently shows the wrong zone map; the `?? 0` fallback then quietly maps unknown positions to zone 1. Works today (interiors span x ∈ [-9, 80]), but it is a copy of layout constants in the UI layer.
mediums.fix: Use the shared helper: `const d = dungeonAt(p.pos.x); const zone = d ? zoneAt(d.doorPos.z) : zoneAt(p.pos.z);` (import dungeonAt from '../sim/data') and delete the literals.

mediums.title: Vendor window scroll position and hovered tooltip are destroyed on every purchase now that the list scrolls
mediums.detail: This branch added `max-height: 68%; overflow-y: auto` to #vendor-window because the new weapon/armor shopkeepers carry long inventories. Every `buyItem` triggers a 'vendor' event which calls `renderVendor()`, which resets `el.innerHTML` — collapsing scrollHeight and clamping scrollTop to 0, and replacing the hovered row so its `mouseleave` never fires (the tooltip stays stuck on screen until another tooltip element is hovered). Buying several items from the bottom of a long vendor list means re-scrolling after every single click, with a frozen tooltip overlay.
mediums.fix: In renderVendor(), capture `const st = el.scrollTop` before rebuilding and restore `el.scrollTop = st` after; call `this.hideTooltip()` at the start of the rebuild. (Longer term: update the price/rows in place instead of innerHTML-resetting the scroll container.)

mediums.title: Offline character name is unvalidated and interpolated into innerHTML (quest text $N, char window title)
mediums.detail: Online character names are validated server-side (`validCharName: /^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z' -]{1,15}$/`), so party frames / trade / context-menu innerHTML paths are safe — verified. But the OFFLINE flow takes the name straight from the input with only `.trim().slice(0, 16)` and passes it to the Sim; hud then injects it via `quest.text.replace(/\$N/g, this.sim.player.name)` into `innerHTML` in renderQuestDetail and renderQuestLog, and into the char-window panel title. A name containing `<`, `>` or `&` (e.g. `<b>Bob`) corrupts the quest dialog markup or executes as self-XSS in the player's own session. Harmless to other players, but a player who types an angle bracket gets visibly broken quest text.
mediums.fix: Apply the same rule client-side for offline names (reject or strip chars not matching /^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z' -]{1,15}$/ before calling startOffline), or HTML-escape the name wherever it is interpolated into innerHTML.
